mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate, sustained above a restrictive threshold, increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative equity market performance over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Oct 2023, the effective FEDFUNDS rate is 5.33%, a multi-decade high.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the FEDFUNDS rate is above 5.0% for a consecutive 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth over a 3-month period reflects a robust economy and strong corporate revenue growth, which translates into positive forward equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent payroll reports through Oct 2023 have shown continued, albeit slowing, job creation.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a rank information coefficient below 0.03 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) coincides with weakening housing starts (HOUST), it signals a divergence in consumer spending away from interest-rate sensitive sectors. This indicates underlying economic fragility and predicts negative returns for the broad market.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 1% and 3-month change in HOUST < -1%
failure_condition: The specified trigger condition does not lead to statistically significant negative returns (p > 0.05) compared to periods where both series are moving in the same direction.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the year-over-year CPI (CPIAUCSL) while the Fed Funds rate is high signals that inflation is being controlled, increasing the probability of a future monetary policy pivot. This 'peak inflation' signal boosts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI has fallen from its 2022 peak while FEDFUNDS remains elevated.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger is active, the 3-month change in YoY CPI is not negatively correlated with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a robust manufacturing and supply side of the economy, translating directly to higher aggregate corporate revenues and positive equity market performance over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in INDPRO has a rank correlation below 0.05 with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) that rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its 12-month low signals a deterioration in the labor market, acting as a leading indicator of recession and causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses a similar logic to detect recessions.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger is active, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic expansion. This signal supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:The latest available GDP print for Q2 2023 showed continued economic expansion.
failure_condition: The sign of quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth does not have a statistically significant positive correlation (p > 0.05) with the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is rising, it creates a policy conflict for the central bank. This 'stagflationary impulse' increases uncertainty, raises risk premia, and drives negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL > 3.5% and 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 3.9% as of Oct 2023, up from 3.4% in Apr 2023.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 does not exhibit a statistically significant negative mean return in the 5 trading days following the trigger condition being met, compared to baseline periods.
